title: Dangerous Storms Hit the Midwest

source.title: Midwest battered by dangerous storms, 1 killed in Des Moines

segments.title: Severe Weather
segments.audioText: Severe weather broke out in the Midwest on Thursday, day three of a multi-day threat.
segments.transcript: Severe weather broke out in the Midwest on Thursday, day three of a multi-day threat.
segments.translationZh: 星期四，中西部爆發惡劣天氣，係多日威脅嘅第三日。

points.label: Severe weather broke out
points.explanationZh: broke out 原本解『爆發』，喺天氣新聞入面即惡劣天氣突然出現。

points.label: in the Midwest on Thursday
points.explanationZh: 地點加時間放喺句尾，聽新聞時可以先抓 Midwest 同 Thursday。

points.label: day three of a multi-day threat
points.explanationZh: day three 即第三日；multi-day threat 係持續幾日嘅威脅。

segments.title: Storm Path
segments.audioText: More than 120 million people are in the path of potentially dangerous storms.
segments.transcript: More than 120 million people are in the path of potentially dangerous storms.
segments.translationZh: 超過一億二千萬人正處於可能有危險嘅風暴路徑上。

points.label: More than 120 million people
points.explanationZh: 聽數字時先抓 More than，再聽 120 million，表示超過一億二千萬。

points.label: are in the path of
points.explanationZh: in the path of 即喺某事物行經路線上，天氣報道常用。

points.label: potentially dangerous storms
points.explanationZh: potentially 即可能地；dangerous storms 指可能帶來風險嘅風暴。

segments.title: Des Moines
segments.audioText: In Des Moines, a man was killed after a tree broke apart and fell on him as storms passed through.
segments.transcript: In Des Moines, a man was killed after a tree broke apart and fell on him as storms passed through.
segments.translationZh: 喺 Des Moines，一棵樹喺風暴經過時斷裂並倒落一名男子身上，令佢喪生。

points.label: In Des Moines
points.explanationZh: Des Moines 係地名，聽到 in 後面接城市名就可以先當作地點。

points.label: a man was killed after
points.explanationZh: was killed 係被動語態；after 後面交代事情點樣發生。

points.label: a tree broke apart and fell on him
points.explanationZh: broke apart 即斷裂；fell on him 即倒落佢身上。

points.label: as storms passed through
points.explanationZh: as 呢度解『當……嘅時候』；passed through 指風暴經過該區。

segments.title: Forecast
segments.audioText: Rob Marciano reports and has the forecast.
segments.transcript: Rob Marciano reports and has the forecast.
segments.translationZh: Rob Marciano 會帶嚟報道同天氣預報。

points.label: Rob Marciano reports
points.explanationZh: 人名加 reports 係新聞常見句式，意思係由某位記者報道。

points.label: has the forecast
points.explanationZh: has the forecast 即『會帶嚟天氣預報』，forecast 係天氣預報。

points.label: reports and has
points.explanationZh: 兩個動作用 and 連接，跟讀時 reports and 可以連住講。

sentences: Severe weather broke out in the Midwest on Thursday, day three of a multi-day threat.
More than 120 million people are in the path of potentially dangerous storms.
In Des Moines, a man was killed after a tree broke apart and fell on him as storms passed through.
Rob Marciano reports and has the forecast.

vocabulary.example: Severe weather can change travel plans quickly.
vocabulary.translationZh: 惡劣天氣可以好快改變出行安排。

vocabulary.example: The storm threat lasted for several days.
The roads may be potentially dangerous tonight.
vocabulary.translationZh: 今晚啲路可能有潛在危險。

vocabulary.example: I checked the forecast before leaving home.
vocabulary.translationZh: 我出門前睇咗天氣預報。

phrases.example: Severe weather broke out in the afternoon.
Several towns are in the path of the storm.
People should prepare for potentially dangerous storms.
The meteorologist has the forecast after the break.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:00 跟讀第一句，將 Severe weather broke out 當成一個起步 chunk。
重播 00:08，先聽寫 More than 120 million people，再補 are in the path of...。
用 in the path of 練替換：Our neighborhood is in the path of the storm.
用兩句英文複述：Severe storms hit the Midwest. More than 120 million people were in the path of dangerous weather.

notes: 今日重點係天氣新聞入面嘅數字、地名同風險用語，聽嘅時候先抓 severe weather、Midwest、120 million。
用時間碼 00:00、00:08、00:16、00:28 分段重聽；每次只聽一個句子，唔好一次過追完整段新聞。
第三句內容較沉重，練習重點放喺 after 同 as 兩個連接詞點樣交代事件次序。
dailyLifePractice 會轉去較輕鬆、實用嘅日常場景：查天氣、改約時間同問店員。

dailyLifePractice.title: Checking the Weather
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：出門前同朋友確認天氣，想自然講可能會落雨或有風暴。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Did you check the weather? It looks like storms may roll in later. Let's bring umbrellas just in case.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Not yet. Is it supposed to rain?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: It looks like storms may roll in later.
lines.zh: 睇落遲啲可能有風暴過嚟。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Let's bring umbrellas just in case.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: roll in 係好自然嘅天氣口語，表示雲、雨、風暴慢慢移入。just in case 即以防萬一，日常非常常用。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 storms 換成 rain、snow 或 heavy traffic。
drills.example: It looks like heavy traffic may roll in later.

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 Let's bring umbrellas just in case 三次。
drills.example: Let's bring umbrellas just in case.

dailyLifePractice.title: Pushing Plans Back
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：天氣差令你出門慢咗，要禮貌同朋友改遲少少。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Can we push our plans back a bit? The weather is slowing me down. I should be there around six-thirty.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Can we push our plans back a bit?
lines.zh: 我哋可唔可以將安排推遲少少？

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Yeah, the weather is slowing me down.
lines.zh: 冇事，係天氣令我慢咗少少。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I should be there around six-thirty.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: push our plans back a bit 聽落自然又有禮貌。The weather is slowing me down 比 I am late because of weather 更口語。

drills.label: Polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 Can we push...back a bit? 改另一個安排。
drills.example: Can we push dinner back a bit?

drills.label: Short response
drills.prompt: 朋友問 Everything okay? 用一句簡短英文回覆。
drills.example: Yeah, traffic is slowing me down.

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking for Help in a Store
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你想買手電筒或電池，準備應付壞天氣，要問店員喺邊度。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Excuse me, where can I find flashlights? Do you also have batteries nearby? Thanks, I appreciate it.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Excuse me, where can I find flashlights?
lines.zh: 唔好意思，手電筒喺邊度可以搵到？

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.en: Aisle seven, near the camping gear.
lines.zh: 第七條通道，露營用品附近。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Do you also have batteries nearby?

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Do you also have...? 係好順口嘅追加問題。I appreciate it 比 thanks 稍微更客氣，但仍然好日常。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 flashlights 換成 bottled water、candles 或 phone chargers。
drills.example: Where can I find bottled water?

drills.label: Role-play
drills.prompt: 你做顧客，店員答 Aisle seven, near the camping gear，你用一句英文回應。
